$comment: FC17.9(e) + D-CSUBSET-LONG-DOUBLE-X87-ARITH (LD-1): the per-format long-double axis, runtime-witnessed on BOTH axes. f64-axis (pe64 x86_64 MSVC + Apple arm64): the axis collapses the typeSpecifiers row to F64, so the whole double substrate serves `long double` with zero new codegen. x87-80 axis (elf64 x86_64 linux): `long double` is the 80-bit x87 extended type — the literals widen double->80-bit extended in rodata, `g_a + g_b` runs on the REAL x87 FPU (fld/fld/faddp/fstp), and `(int)sum` truncates via fld/fisttp/mov (LD-1). Exercises: the `long double` declaration multiset, 20.0L/22.0L l-suffixed FLOAT literals (the CRITICAL-1 suffix-shape separation keeps integer 20L distinct), UAC arithmetic, sizeof (the `>= sizeof(double)` guard holds on both axes: equal on f64, 16-vs-8 on x87-80), and (int) conversion. Operands route through MUTABLE GLOBALS so the release arm proves real runtime arithmetic, not a fold (the c11_atomic anti-fold precedent). The ieee128 formats (linux-arm64) remain ABSENT until D-CSUBSET-LONG-DOUBLE-IEEE128-ARITH lands; the still-walled F80 CALL BOUNDARIES (Arg/Call-result/Return-operand — LD-4) are pinned in the unit suites (F80{Arg,CallResult,ReturnOperand}BoundaryWallsFailLoud in tests/lir/test_mir_to_lir.cpp; the in-function arithmetic + Store LOWER, witnessed by F80ArithmeticAndStoreLowerToX87Sequence + F80StoreValueLowersToX87Copy). exit = (int)(20.0L + 22.0L) = 42.
